title: Reimburse Ikigai Info Governance Action Deposit.

rationale.summary: Vote: NEEDS_MORE_INFO. For this TreasuryWithdrawals (active), critical information needed for a responsible directional vote is still missing. Decisive reason: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty. Weighed against it: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests 103,000 ADA from the treasury to reimburse a 100,000 ADA governance action deposit plus 3,000 ADA for lost staking rewards." though 5 review blocker(s) remain open. What would enable a directional vote: Provide: Deep research dossier is required for treasury actions before directional voting; Provide: Complete proposal summary, budget analysis, feasibility, risks, alternatives, and failure-mode sections. Residual uncertainty: Dossier completeness not confirmed for this treasury proposal. Confidence context: score 0.00; confidence 20.0%.
rationale.summary_raw: Directional voting is blocked until dossier quality gates pass.
top_fixes: Provide: Deep research dossier is required for treasury actions before directional voting.
Provide: Complete proposal summary, budget analysis, feasibility, risks, alternatives, and failure-mode sections.
Provide: Missing dossier section: budget analysis
missing_evidence: Deep research dossier is required for treasury actions before directional voting.
Complete proposal summary, budget analysis, feasibility, risks, alternatives, and failure-mode sections.
Missing dossier section: community impact analysis

sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Reimburse the Ikigai Info governance action deposit and compensate claimed lost staking rewards.
Recipient: Ikigai Info governance action submitter
Deliverables: On-chain treasury withdrawal paid directly to the intended recipient upon enactment
Deadline/expiry: instantly performed as part of the governance action's enactment on-chain

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests 103,000 ADA from the treasury to reimburse a 100,000 ADA governance action deposit plus 3,000 ADA for lost staking rewards.
Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The original Ikigai Info governance action was a symbolic action thanking contributors and expressing hope for Cardano's future.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The submitter was unable to recover the 100,000 ADA deposit because of a Cardano node bug that permitted an unregistered stake key to be used in the governance action.
Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The document says the community expressed significant sentiment that the deposit should be reimbursed.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The Cardano in Oceania initiative included this reimbursement in a budget Info governance action, but that governance action was not approved.
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, medium materiality): The additional 3,000 ADA is described as compensation for lost staking rewards at a rate of 2% per annum.
missing: Independent evidence for: The original Ikigai Info governance action was a symbolic action thanking contributors and expressing hope for Cardano's future.
Independent evidence for: The submitter was unable to recover the 100,000 ADA deposit because of a Cardano node bug that permitted an unregistered stake key to be used in the governance action.
Independent evidence for: The document says the community expressed significant sentiment that the deposit should be reimbursed.
Independent evidence for: The Cardano in Oceania initiative included this reimbursement in a budget Info governance action, but that governance action was not approved.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests 103,000 ADA from the treasury to reimburse a 100,000 ADA governance action deposit plus 3,000 ADA for lost staking rewards." though 5 review blocker(s) remain open.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The original Ikigai Info governance action was a symbolic action thanking contributors and expressing hope for Cardano's future." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.

decision_contract.composite_formula: 40% ecosystem benefit + 25% delivery confidence + 20% cost efficiency + 15% downside protection - 0.50
decision_contract.yes_rule: All quality floors, independently verified evidence, verified NCL, and portfolio capacity must pass.
decision_contract.no_rule: Requires affirmative independently verified waste/harm plus ecosystem benefit below 55%; missing evidence alone is never NO.
decision_contract.portfolio_gate: Verified on-chain Net Change Limit and opportunity-cost portfolio eligibility are mandatory for treasury direction.
